apple_os_naming_convention.claim: Apple transitioned to year-based OS naming starting with WWDC 2025, so the third major release of visionOS was called 'visionOS 26' rather than 'visionOS 3'.

provenance.definition: Apple transitioned to year-based OS naming starting with WWDC 2025, so the third major release of visionOS was called 'visionOS 26' rather than 'visionOS 3'.
provenance.level_fallback_reason: all levels sparse (total 1 usable case(s)); rate is wide by construction

cases.description: Apple announced visionOS 26 at WWDC 2025, using year-based naming for the first time.
cases.basis_quote: Apple today announced visionOS 26, the next major version of the operating system for Apple Vision Pro.

apple_vision_pro_sales_decline.claim: Apple Vision Pro sales have been low and the platform is considered abandoned by Apple internally.

provenance.definition: Apple Vision Pro sales have been low and the platform is considered abandoned by Apple internally.
provenance.level_fallback_reason: all levels sparse (total 2 usable case(s)); rate is wide by construction

cases.description: MacRumors reported that the M5 Vision Pro refresh failed to revive interest, with total sales only approximately 1.2 million units.
cases.basis_quote: The M5 refresh of Apple Vision Pro failed to revive consumer interest, with total sales reaching only approximately 1.2 million units.

cases.description: Apple stopped work on Vision Pro, redistributed the team to Siri, cancelled Vision Air, and shifted focus to AI smart glasses.
cases.basis_quote: Apple has reportedly stopped work on Vision Pro, redistributed the team to other projects, cancelled the Vision Air project, and shifted focus toward AI-powered smart glasses.

wwdc_annual_os_announcement_pattern.claim: Apple has historically announced major new versions of its operating systems at WWDC each June.

provenance.definition: Apple has historically announced major new versions of its operating systems at WWDC each June.
provenance.level_fallback_reason: all levels sparse (total 2 usable case(s)); rate is wide by construction

cases.description: Apple announced iOS 19, iPadOS 19, macOS 16, watchOS 12, visionOS 26 at WWDC 2025.
cases.basis_quote: Apple today announced iOS 19, iPadOS 19, macOS 16, watchOS 12, and visionOS 26.

cases.description: Apple announced iOS 18, iPadOS 18, macOS 15, watchOS 11, visionOS 2 at WWDC 2024.
cases.basis_quote: Apple today announced iOS 18, iPadOS 18, macOS 15, watchOS 11, and visionOS 2.

outcome_reference_class.quantity: Apple announces visionOS 27 (or a successor major version to visionOS 26) at WWDC 2026

provenance.definition: A public announcement by Apple of a new major version of visionOS that succeeds visionOS 26, occurring between May 6, 2026 and June 12, 2026, either during the WWDC 2026 keynote or via an official Apple Newsroom press release.
provenance.inclusion_criteria: The announcement must explicitly name a new major version (e.g., visionOS 27, visionOS 3) intended to replace visionOS 26.
provenance.exclusion_criteria: Minor updates like visionOS 26.5 or 26.6 are excluded.
provenance.level_fallback_reason: all levels sparse (total 2 usable case(s)); rate is wide by construction

cases.basis_quote: Apple today announced visionOS 26, the next major version of the operating system for Apple Vision Pro.
Apple today announced visionOS 2, the next major version of the operating system for Apple Vision Pro.

apple_wwdc_keynote.consequence_of_nonparticipation: No announcement of a new major visionOS version would resolve the question as No.
